content: We can frame Self-Refine as a single-perspective iterative bootstrapping primitive, and integrate multi-perspective reflection mechanisms from LLM self-reflection research to build a hierarchical self-refinement framework that requires no extra training or labeled supervision. This framework lets an LLM first refine an output from its initial problem-solving perspective, then pull inconsistent alternative perspectives from its own parametric knowledge to catch blind spots missed in single-perspective iteration.
